summary: Replaced the project-specific fabs_inline pattern with the existing ABS(x) macro across 27 files, deleted duplicated inline absolute-value helpers from src/MSL/math.h and local modules, and cleaned up a few matching-sensitive expressions while preserving absolute-value semantics. The change primarily consolidated math/header usage and improved decomp matching in several unmatched functions, with the decomp.dev bot reporting improvements in ftCo_8009F834, ftAction_80072B3C, and Camera_800307D0.

change_classification.primary_type: header/math macro consolidation for decomp matching

what_changed: Removed static inline f32 fabs_inline(f32 x) from src/MSL/math.h, leaving #define ABS(x) ((x) < 0 ? -(x) : (x)) as the shared absolute-value helper.

Removed a local M_PI/ABS/fabs_inline block from src/melee/ft/ftcommon.c and replaced it with #include <math.h>, then converted many ftCommon friction, velocity, and drift comparisons from fabs_inline(...) to ABS(...).

Converted many fighter input and movement checks to ABS(...), including stick deadzones, run/runbrake velocity thresholds, jump horizontal speed clamping, fall drift animation selection, item throw input thresholds, and boss position correction.
Converted character-specific code in Ness, Peach, Pikachu, and Link from fabs_inline or local fabs helper functions to ABS(...).
Deleted the local fabs_inline_ness helper in ftNs_SpecialHi.c and used ABS(pos->x - pair->x) / ABS(pos->y - pair->y) in check_distance.
Deleted the local nested_sum_fabs helper in ftPk_SpecialLw.c and expressed its result directly as ABS(fp->cur_pos.y + ABS(pika_attr->xBC) - vec.y).
Changed ftPk_SpecialLw_8012765C from direct gobj->user_data access to GET_FIGHTER(gobj), a small style/consistency cleanup adjacent to the ABS conversion.
Updated camera math in src/melee/cm/camera.c, replacing fabs_inline calls with ABS in Camera_8002958C, compute_edge, and Camera_800307D0.

Camera_800307D0 also had matching-sensitive expression cleanup: inlined HSD_CObjGetAspect into the half_fov calculation, changed PAD_STACK(8) to PAD_STACK(0x10), and collapsed nested ABS threshold checks into a single condition.
A decomp.dev bot comment reported 3 unmatched-function improvements: ftCo_8009F834 from 24.77% to 27.00%, ftAction_80072B3C from 52.63% to 94.44%, and Camera_800307D0 from 87.50% to 88.75%.
smart_moves: Consolidated an obviously duplicated fake/helper absolute-value implementation into the existing ABS macro instead of keeping module-local inline functions with near-identical bodies.
Removed the explicit TODO in math.h questioning whether fabs_inline was fake by making ABS the canonical spelling, reducing future uncertainty for decomp authors.
Used macro expansion to preserve the source-level expression shape decompilers often need, especially for simple comparisons like ABS(x) < threshold and ABS(x) > threshold.
Eliminated local helper functions whose names encoded file-specific workarounds, such as fabs_inline_ness and nested_sum_fabs, making the decompiled code more idiomatic and searchable.
In ftPk_SpecialLw, replaced a helper whose parameters partly duplicated recomputable expressions with a single direct ABS expression, reducing noise around the actual game logic.
Paired mechanical ABS replacement with small matching-sensitive cleanup where evidence showed it helped, especially in Camera_800307D0.
decomp_lessons: Prefer the shared ABS(x) macro in math.h over inventing static inline fabs_inline variants when matching absolute-value idioms in Melee C code.
If a helper exists only to force a specific inline absolute-value shape, test whether a macro gives the same or better codegen before adding another function-like workaround.
Local duplicate definitions of common math helpers make the codebase harder to search and can hide project-wide matching improvements; centralize them when possible.
Expression shape matters: direct ABS(a + ABS(b) - c) can be preferable to a named helper if the original codegen corresponds to nested comparisons/negations rather than a real call.
Small stack and temporary-variable changes can interact with macro substitutions. Camera_800307D0 needed PAD_STACK and expression reshaping alongside replacing fabs_inline.
Bot-reported percentage changes are useful evidence for broad mechanical cleanups; even a seemingly stylistic macro replacement produced measurable improvements in unmatched functions.
naming_conventions: Use ABS(x) for absolute-value macro spelling; do not introduce new fabs_inline or fabs_inline_<module> helpers without strong evidence.
Use #include <math.h> to obtain shared math macros/constants rather than redefining ABS or M_PI in individual C files.
Prefer GET_FIGHTER(gobj) over direct gobj->user_data access where surrounding code follows that convention, as seen in ftPk_SpecialLw_8012765C.
Keep temporary names that describe absolute values when useful, such as abs_lstick_x, abs_lstick_angle, and abs_x_diff, but compute them with ABS(...).
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replacing inline functions with ABS(x) can alter the compiler's emitted compare/negate expression shape enough to improve matching.
For nested absolute-value logic, write the expression directly with nested ABS calls rather than introducing a helper that may affect inlining, temporaries, or register allocation.
In Camera_800307D0, inlining HSD_CObjGetAspect into the half_fov expression and adjusting PAD_STACK from 8 to 0x10 accompanied a small matching improvement reported by the bot.
Collapsing nested threshold checks like if (ABS(forward.x) > 1e-4 && ABS(forward.z) > 1e-4) can match better than nested if blocks when the generated control flow expects one combined condition.
Mechanical substitutions should still be checked against generated code; the bot report showed improvements but the PR dump contains no human explanation for every local expression adjustment.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The only issue comment was from decomp.dev bot, reporting improvements in three unmatched functions: ftCo_8009F834, ftAction_80072B3C, and Camera_800307D0.
The PR merged quickly, about six minutes after creation, but the slice does not include human rationale or reviewer discussion.
follow_up_queries: Search for remaining fabs_inline or fabs_inline_* helpers after PR 1761.
Search for local #define ABS or #define M_PI blocks outside math.h that should be replaced by #include <math.h>.
Audit other simple static inline math helpers that may be fake and better represented as macros.
Check Camera_800307D0 history for why cm_804D7EE8 and 1e-4 are interchangeable or whether that constant should be named/documented.
Look for direct gobj->user_data accesses in fighter code that should use GET_FIGHTER(gobj) for consistency.
